headline: BofA resets Salesforce stock price target with sobering AI verdict
summary: Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) just picked up one of the more unusual analyst calls of 2026, and it landed at a price the market wasn't ready for. headline: How Investors Are Reacting To Salesforce (CRM) After Bank of America Flags AI Monetization Challenges
summary: In mid-May 2026, Bank of America reinstated coverage of Salesforce with an Underperform rating, arguing that the company faces a structural reset as AI reshapes its core CRM and agentic offerings. The bank’s critique that Salesforce may be shifting from a high-growth story to a mature cash generator highlights a tension between its aggressive AI investments and questions over how effectively those tools will be monetized. We’ll now examine how Bank of America’s skepticism about Salesforce’s...

headline: AusNet rolls out unified customer connections platform to fast‑track services as grid demand rises
summary: AusNet, Australia's largest diversified energy network business, has collaborated with IBM Consulting (NYSE: IBM) and Salesforce to transform how customers and electricians request and manage electricity connections. The new single, unified platform gives AusNet the ability to deliver faster, more transparent services for customers and electricians while scaling to meet rising demand — a timely, future-ready transformation as Australia prepares for increasing grid pressure driven by electrificat

headline: How a book convinced Arundhati Bhattacharya, one of India’s most powerful bankers, to try working for a U.S. tech company
summary: Under Arundhati Bhattacharya, Salesforce’s South Asia headcount grew from 2,500 to over 18,000; India is now one of Salesforce’s fastest growing markets.

content.title: How a book convinced Arundhati Bhattacharya, one of India’s most powerful bankers, to try working for a U.S. tech company
content.summary: Under Arundhati Bhattacharya, Salesforce’s South Asia headcount grew from 2,500 to over 18,000; India is now one of Salesforce’s fastest growing markets.

thumbnail.caption: When Arundhati Bhattacharya stepped down as the State Bank of India (SBI)’s chairperson in 2017, she never envisaged an American tech firm as her next gig. “Finance to tech was a move that was never planned,” Salesforce's South Asia CEO tells Fortune. “When this role came about, I sat on it for about five to six months, trying to make up my mind on whether to start in a new area after so many years, and after retiring.”

headline: US software stocks attempt a rebound as investors reassess AI risks
summary: U.S. software stocks headed toward their fourth consecutive session of gains on Tuesday, attempting a comeback after being battered ‌for much of the year on fears of disruption from artificial intelligence.  The iShares Expanded Tech‑Software Sector ETF rose 1.1%, hitting its highest level since January.  Workday, ServiceNow and Salesforce ‌rose between 3.7% and ⁠4.3%.

content.title: US software stocks attempt a rebound as investors reassess AI risks
content.summary: U.S. software stocks headed toward their fourth consecutive session of gains on Tuesday, attempting a comeback after being battered ‌for much of the year on fears of disruption from artificial intelligence.  The iShares Expanded Tech‑Software Sector ETF rose 1.1%, hitting its highest level since January.  Workday, ServiceNow and Salesforce ‌rose between 3.7% and ⁠4.3%.

headline: JAGGAER Appoints Procurement Leader Craig Cuffie to Board of Directors
summary: RESEARCH TRIANGLE PARK, N.C., May 19, 2026--JAGGAER, a global leader in AI-powered source-to-pay and supplier collaboration software, today announced the appointment of Craig Cuffie to its Board of Directors. The move strengthens JAGGAER’s ability to help enterprises navigate growing supply chain complexity and make faster, more intelligent spend decisions.

content.title: JAGGAER Appoints Procurement Leader Craig Cuffie to Board of Directors
